review_notes: The functions are relevant to the control SA-9(6) as they address the management and security of cryptographic keys within Kubernetes. The naming conventions are mostly consistent with the standards, but there is room for improvement in clarity and specificity. The functions correctly map to Kubernetes security features related to secrets and etcd encryption.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_secret_rotation_policy_configured' to 'k8s_secret_rotation_policy_enabled' for consistency with other function names that use 'enabled'.
title: External System Services - External system services | organization - controlled cryptographic keys maintain
step1_reasoning: The control SA-9(6) focuses on the management and rotation of cryptographic keys, which is applicable to Kubernetes in the context of managing secrets and etcd encryption. Kubernetes supports encryption at rest for secrets and etcd, which aligns with the requirement to maintain control over cryptographic keys. Additionally, configuring a rotation policy for secrets can be implemented through external secret management solutions integrated with Kubernetes. Ensuring etcd communication is secured with TLS further supports the control's objectives.
